title: Collections
scan: What you will learn: the Strategy pattern applied to sort and filter — how to manage multiple sort/filter algorithms without if-else chains
Why it matters: every content-heavy AEM site has a collection component — how it is sorted and filtered determines performance, testability, and author control
a sort strategy is a pure function that takes two items and returns a comparison value — any sort that does anything else is wrong
filter composition (AND vs OR logic) is a business rule not an implementation detail — get it in writing before coding

blocks.html: oment you have a group of things, you need two operations forever: sort (put them in order) and filter (keep only what matters). The mistake is scattering that logic across the views that consume the data.

sections.title: Put sort and filter behind a manager

blocks.html: Put sorting and filtering behind a single manager. Views ask it for the slice they need; they never re-implement the rules. Swap a sort comparator or a filter predicate in one place — that's the Strategy pattern earning its keep.

sections.title: Strategy registries and the sort-location decision

blocks.html: DEPT® field pattern — implement sort and filter as separate strategy registries. A filter registry (keyed by filter name, value: predicate function) plus a sort registry (keyed by sort name, value: comparator function) composes to a full collection management system with zero hard-coded combinations. Adding a new sort is adding one function to one object.

Sorting on the client after a server-side paginated response. The sort applies to the current page only; users see inconsistent ordering as they paginate. This is not a bug that appears in testing — it appears when the collection has more than one page and a user notices the anomaly. Decide sort location (client or server) before API design, not after.

blocks.label: Architect's Review · Collections
items: Where is sort logic — duplicated, or behind a manager?
Where is filter logic — the same?
Can comparators and predicates be swapped without touching consumers?
Is pagination part of the collection, or bolted on at the view?
